title: Any known issues with calling opencv code from multiple threads under OS X ?
body:
Hello!
I have run into a very difficult-to-comprehend problem.
I am calling OpenCV code from the JVM (through the javacpp project https://github.com/bytedeco/javacpp ). In my program, I have a process which takes several images and runs some simple image processing commands on each (rgb2gray, some erosion & dilation).
Crucially, everything is fine _so long as my program only calls opencv code in serial_. The second that I start processing each image in parallel, my program has deterministic behavior. Eventually, it always receives a SIGSEV. _This happens even when I set the number of threads in openCV to 0_.The Java signal handler catches this, prints out the following, and dies:
```
# C  [libopencv_imgproc.3.0.dylib+0x36490]  cv::CvtColorLoop_Invoker<cv::RGB2Gray<unsigned char> >::operator()(cv::Range const&) const+0x70
```
So, _on os x_, it is still trying to do the RGB2Gray conversion in parallel (that's what `CvtColorLoop_Invoker` does to the extent of my C++ knowledge and code reviews over the past few days).
However... here's where stuff gets interesting. If I execute the same code under linux, I have no problems when running in serial or in parallel.
This fact leads me to believe that there is something wrong with either (1) open cv not respecting serial processing on OS X or (2) something wrong with grand central dispatch in OS X itself.
I am not a C++ dev so I need a lot of help in tracking down this problem. I am very willing to put in time and effort to track down the root cause of this behavior, find a fix, and push it back upstream.
Thank you for any and all help in advance!

title: GStreamer + HLS stream taking up a massive amount of CPU
body:
Hello all! Working on a rather tough problem lately that I've having a hard time wrapping my head around.
I have an HLS livestream I'm reading from twitch.tv. I want to grab one frame per second from the livestream, and save it locally. But, I need to stay real time. So, when I save an image from the stream, that image should be very close to what is actually live.
This isn't very difficult via GStreamer on the command line.
But, I actually need to process the stream in Python (because I'm doing some other processing on the frame before I save it). OpenCV actually has a gstreamer integration that can be found here.
As for the code itself, it's quite simple:
```
cap = cv2.VideoCapture( 
    f"souphttpsrc is_live=true location={hls_stream_link} ! hlsdemux ! 
    queue ! decodebin ! videorate ! video/x-raw,framerate=1/1 ! 
    videoconvert ! appsink max-buffers=1 drop=true sync=false", 
cv2.CAP_GSTREAMER) 

while True: 
    success, frame = cap.read() 
    save_frame(frame) 
    time.sleep(1.0) 
```
This code is doing exactly what I want it to do. Saving one frame per second and staying close to real time. This is why I do: max-buffers=1 drop=true sync=false. I also have a one second sleep, since I only need to read one frame per second.
The issue is, the above code takes up 250% of my machine's CPU (tested on Windows, Linux, and OS X). I'm attempting to understand why this is the case and what I can do to reduce CPU usage.
I'm having a hard time understanding why decoding a single frame from an HLS stream leads to such high CPU usage. I've tried Python multithreading and using a special queue to save frames on a separate process and then read from that queue.

title: Stereo pair sample for testing calibrated stereo setup?
body:
##### System information (version)
- OpenCV => 4.1.0
- Operating System / Platform =>Ubuntu 18.04
I want to test out the pipeline for stereo camera calibration and depth map estimation. I used the OpenCV sample (samples/data/left01.jpg – left14.jpg and right ...) for calibration. Is there any pair of images on which I could test depth map estimation after the stereo set has been calibrated with the **given example**?
##### Relevant Documentation
The [camera calibration tutorial](https://docs.opencv.org/4.1.0/dd/d53/tutorial_py_depthmap.html) uses the same sample (samples/data/left01.jpg – left14.jpg and right ...) for calibration as I did. Next, it is unclear whether the pair of images from [depth map from stereo images tutorial](https://docs.opencv.org/4.1.0/dd/d53/tutorial_py_depthmap.html) is actually the pair of images taken with the same stereo camera that was calibrated in the previous tutorial. Also, this tutorial only shows how to compute disparity but not actually estimate the depth.
It'd be great if the depth map tutorial had a stereo image pair, so that depth from this pair could be estimated using camera parameters from the calibration tutorial.

title: Accuracy of the principal point is very bad in calibrateCamera
body:
OpenCV python version: 4.7.0.72
Operating System / Platform: windows11
Python version: 3.10.10
Accuracy of the principal point(cx,cy) is very bad in calibrateCamera. I tested 4 sets of images, each set includes over 40 images captured from various angles, but the result I got differs a lot. cx is ```[9.37599252e+02,9.39782917e+02,9.36069872e+02,9.53738576e+02]``` and cy is ```[5.07172169e+02,5.22300532e+02,5.28459216e+02,5.38614800e+02]```.
you can get original data [here](https://github.com/00ffcc/calibratecameraimg), my camera's resolution is 1920*1080.
please told me how to increase the accuracy, thx.
download data [here](https://github.com/00ffcc/calibratecameraimg), and use the program to calibrate.
```python
import cv2
import numpy as np
import glob
criteria = (cv2.TERM_CRITERIA_EPS + cv2.TERM_CRITERIA_MAX_ITER, 30, 0.001)
w = 11  
h = 8
objp = np.zeros((w*h,3), np.float32)
objp[:,:2] = np.mgrid[0:w,0:h].T.reshape(-1,2)
objpoints = []
imgpoints = []

images = glob.glob('img/*.jpg')
for fname in images:
    img = cv2.imread(fname)
    gray = cv2.cvtColor(img,cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)
    ret, corners = cv2.findChessboardCorners(gray, (w,h),None)
    if ret == True:
        cv2.cornerSubPix(gray,corners,(11,11),(-1,-1),criteria)
        objpoints.append(objp)
        imgpoints.append(corners)
        cv2.drawChessboardCorners(img, (w,h), corners, ret)
        cv2.imshow('findCorners',img)
        cv2.waitKey(100)
    else:
        print(fname)
cv2.destroyAllWindows()
ret, mtx, dist, rvecs, tvecs = cv2.calibrateCamera(objpoints, imgpoints, gray.shape[::-1], None, None)
print (("ret:"),ret)
print (("mtx:\n"),mtx)
print (("dist:\n"),dist)
print (("rvecs:\n"),rvecs)
print (("tvecs:\n"),tvecs)
```

title: `cv2.VideoCapture` does not raise exception immediately after an error occurs
body:
OpenCV python version: 4.6.0.66
Operating System: Windows 11
Python version: 3.11
If a camera stream is not found or a camera is offline (unreachable IP address) opencv will throw an connection error connection of `-138` and softlock the main thread for around 30 seconds until an exception is thrown.
For some reason after the error is encountered it takes opencv a very long time before realizing this and actually throwing an exception.
At times no exception is even thrown even with `cv2.VideoCapture.setExceptionMode(True)` set
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/49711232/215896217-ae11b028-faac-4350-b7b7-63f7de8b0645.png)
```python
import cv2
from datetime import datetime
while True:
    try:
        print(f"{datetime.now().strftime('%H:%M:%S')} || Trying to connect to camera")
        camera = cv2.VideoCapture("http://10.0.0.114")
        camera.setExceptionMode(True)
        if camera.isOpened():
            print("Camera connected!")
            break
        print(f"{datetime.now().strftime('%H:%M:%S')} || Failed to connect to camera, no exception was thrown")
    except:
        print(f"{datetime.now().strftime('%H:%M:%S')} || Failed to connect to camera, exception was thrown")  # wont run
```

title: Request: alpha image controlled two-image composite
body:
Compositing using a weighted mask to control the blend between two images (of possibly different size) is a common task in image processing.
```
Specify the two images to blend (larger background and smaller to be blend inserted)

Crop the larger image to the size of the smaller

Blend (doing float processing) the two using a mask image the size of the smaller according to:

weight is a simple constant between 0 and 1.
composite = weight*mask/255 * smaller_image + (1-weight*mask/255) * crop_of_larger_image

Then insert the composite into a copy of the larger image as the result.

```
See for example https://stackoverflow.com/questions/76358834/png-transparency-with-opencv-in-python/76360541#76360541

title: The rectified image  with the pinhole model  8 parameters will have curved boundary
body:
OpenCV version: 4.9.0.80
Operating System / Platform:  windows10
Python version: 3.10.13
we use the pinhole model  8 parameters to do calibration.
When calibrated with 8 parameters, the corrected graph will have a boundary,if we use 5 parameters, it has no boundary.
some calibration file will have such case with 8 parameters while others will not.
[calibration_demo.zip](https://github.com/opencv/opencv/files/15228554/calibration_demo.zip)

title: openCV versions vastly differ in size
body:
This is a template helping you to create an issue which can be processes as quickly as possible. Feel free to add additional information or remove not relevant points if you do not need them.
If you have a question rather than reporting a bug please go to http://answers.opencv.org where you get much faster responses.
### Please state the information for your system
- OpenCV version: 2.4 / 3.x
- Host OS: Windows 8
### In which part of the OpenCV library you got the issue?
installation
Two different methods are discussed for installing openCV here
http://mathalope.co.uk/2015/05/07/opencv-python-how-to-install-opencv-python-package-to-anaconda-windows/
this is the 'standard' way of installing openCV
conda install -c https://conda.binstar.org/menpo opencv
but an alternative way is here
basically,  download the openCV binary and copy the cv2.pyd file to the Anaconda\Lib\site-packages directory.
The standard way puts a cv2.pyd file that is 2.0 to 2.4MB (depends on the version you pick)
The cv2.pyd from the download is 44MB!
This makes me a little uneasy. Is there any possibility of getting an explanation from this forum as to what accounts for the difference?

title: IPP: cmake chooses the wrong architecture of libippicv.a on JETSON AGX XAVIER
body:
##### System information (version)
- OpenCV => 4.5.2 and 3.4.14
- Operating System / Platform => Ubuntu 18.04.5 LTS / JETSON AGX XAVIER
- Compiler => gcc version 7.5.0 (Ubuntu/Linaro 7.5.0-3ubuntu1~18.04), Target: aarch64-linux-gnu
- Cmake => cmake version 3.10.2
The cmake process download the 32 bits architecture version of the IPPICV library despite the rest of the compilation being X86_64. cmake downloads : "ippicv_2020_lnx_ia32_20191018_general.tgz" instead of "ippicv_2020_lnx_intel64_20191018_general.tgz"
This causes the build to fail during the linking of the library.
```
[ 52%] Linking CXX shared library ../../lib/libopencv_core.so
/usr/bin/ld: ../../3rdparty/ippicv/ippicv_lnx/icv/lib/ia32/libippicv.a(ippcpufeatures.o): Relocations in generic ELF (EM: 3)
../../3rdparty/ippicv/ippicv_lnx/icv/lib/ia32/libippicv.a: error adding symbols: File in wrong format
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
modules/core/CMakeFiles/opencv_core.dir/build.make:2348: recipe for target 'lib/libopencv_core.so.3.4.14' failed
make[2]: *** [lib/libopencv_core.so.3.4.14] Error 1
CMakeFiles/Makefile2:1928: recipe for target 'modules/core/CMakeFiles/opencv_core.dir/all' failed
make[1]: *** [modules/core/CMakeFiles/opencv_core.dir/all] Error 2
Makefile:162: recipe for target 'all' failed
make: *** [all] Error 2
```
Flash the JETSON AGX XAVIER with the latest image (Ubuntu 18.04) using the Nvidia SDK Manager, Install the tools in the manager.
Open a terminal and type
```
#create a folder workspace
mkdir workspace
cd workspace

# Compiler
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install cmake
sudo apt-get install g++

# Dependencies
mkdir dependencies && cd dependencies
dependencies_folder=$(pwd)

# Eigen3
sudo apt-get install libeigen3-dev

# Boost & Python3-dev
sudo apt-get install libboost-all-dev

# OpenSSL
sudo apt-get install libssl-dev

# FFMPEG
sudo apt-get install ffmpeg

# For opencv
sudo apt-get install libgtk2.0-dev
sudo apt-get install pkg-config

# OpenCV (4.5.2 or 3.4.14)
wget -O opencv_contrib.zip https://github.com/opencv/opencv_contrib/archive/4.5.2.zip
unzip opencv_contrib.zip

git clone https://github.com/opencv/opencv.git
mv opencv opencv4 && cd opencv4
git checkout 4.5.2 # or 3.4.14

mkdir build && cd build
cmake -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=$dependencies_folder/opencv4_release \
-D CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=RELEASE \
-D BUILD_EXAMPLES=OFF \
-D BUILD_opencv_apps=OFF \
-D BUILD_DOCS=OFF \
-D BUILD_PERF_TESTS=OFF \
-D BUILD_TESTS=OFF \
-D WITH_TBB=ON \
-D WITH_OPENMP=ON \
-D WITH_IPP=ON \
-D WITH_NVCUVID=ON \
-D WITH_CUDA=ON \
-D WITH_CSTRIPES=ON \
-D WITH_OPENCL=ON \
-D OPENCV_EXTRA_MODULES_PATH=$dependencies_folder/opencv_contrib-4.5.2/modules \
..
make -j12
```
If in the file "3rdparty/ippicv/ippicv.cmake" I change line 15 with
```
if(X86_64 OR TRUE)
```
it works.

title: intersectConvexConvex sometimes fails to give the correct result
body:
OpenCV python version: 4.6.0
OS: Ubuntu 20.04
Python 3.8.10
As described here also https://github.com/opencv/opencv/issues/10044 `intersectConvexConvex` seems to sometimes be returning the wrong answer. I'm opening a new issues because the other one is closed and has not been reopened based on latest comments. Note that unlike the linked issue, I have found that the two polygons need not be totally disjoint for the error to arise. See the Python snippet for a test that shows this.
Here is a Python snippet to reproduce the error:
```python
import cv2
import numpy as np

poly_1 = np.array([
    0.29191044, 1.2570167,
    0.037910465, 1.2571087,
    0.037893265, 1.2571062,
    0.037817374, 1.0475562,
    0.29181734, 1.0474641,
    0.29183453, 1.0474666,
]).reshape(-1, 2)

poly_2 = np.array([
    0.2784909, 1.0457377,
    0.27847248, 1.0457454,
    0.024497004, 1.0492755,
    0.02158466, 0.83974582,
    0.021603081, 0.83973807,
    0.27557856, 0.83620799,
]).reshape(-1, 2)
 
area, intersection = cv2.intersectConvexConvex(poly_1.astype(np.float32), poly_2.astype(np.float32))

print(area)

# Both polygons are convex.
print(cv2.isContourConvex(poly_1.astype(np.float32).reshape(-1, 1, 2)))
print(cv2.isContourConvex(poly_2.astype(np.float32).reshape(-1, 1, 2)))

# Note that one of the points of poly_1 lies inside poly_2
for point in poly_1:
    print(cv2.pointPolygonTest(poly_2.astype(np.float32).reshape(-1, 1, 2), point.astype(np.float32), False))

img = np.zeros(shape=(1000, 1000, 3), dtype=np.uint8)
cv2.polylines(img, [(poly_1 * 500).astype(np.int32).reshape(-1, 1, 2)], True, (255, 0, 0), thickness=3)
cv2.polylines(img, [(poly_2 * 500).astype(np.int32).reshape(-1, 1, 2)], True, (0, 0, 255), thickness=3)
cv2.polylines(img, [(intersection * 500).astype(np.int32).reshape(-1, 1, 2)], True, (0, 255, 0), thickness=1)
cv2.imshow("window", img)
cv2.waitKey()
```
And here is a screen snip of the output. Notice the green rectangle which is supposed to represent the intersection.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/16543381/201987975-fd949f2e-a6a7-484a-b2c2-652c1435d681.png)

- When i using ThreadPool,
```python
import cv2
import time
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor as PoolExecutor


def open_capture(i):
    capture = cv2.VideoCapture()
    begin_time = time.time()
    capture.open(
        'http://devimages.apple.com/iphone/samples/bipbop/gear1/prog_index.m3u8')
    if capture.isOpened():
        print(f'[Open Capture {i}] :{time.time()-begin_time}s')


if __name__ == '__main__':
    pool = PoolExecutor(10)
    pool.submit(open_capture, 1)
    pool.submit(open_capture, 2)
    pool.shutdown()
```

title: cv2 much slower as you increase number of cores. Server and Personal Laptop comparison.
body:
##### System information (version)
Server :
- OpenCV => 3.3.1
- Operating System / Platform => Ubuntu 64 Bit
- Using Python Anaconda fresh install
Personal Laptop :
- OpenCV => 3.1
- Operating System / Platform => Ubuntu 64 Bit
- Python Anaconda but quite an old install. Works perfectly.
Running a python script on server with 8 Intel kabylake cores; (2 Nvidia TI GPUs) was slower than my personal laptop (4 haswell? cores) ! Profiling the code told me the problem was mostly with the cv2 functions and can be seen massively with `cv2.findContours` function.
I tried limiting python to 1 core using
`taskset -c 1 python <program-name>.py`
and the server blew my personal PC away ( **Almost 2.x times faster**).
Allowing two cores using `taskset -c 1,2 python ...` **massively hit performance of program on the server** while reducing performance on my laptop (but not nearly as much as on the server).
On two cores my server was **1.5times slower** than my personal PC.
I have given an example with `cv2.findContours` below :

title: Strange bug in opencv C++ when accessing pixel value of a Mat near edges
OpenCV GStreamer VideoWriter write hangs in a Python multiprocessing Process
body:
OpenCV Python 4.9.0
Python 3.11.2
Raspberry Pi 4
Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)
I'm working on real-time image processing using a Raspberry Pi camera. To optimize processing times, I'm trying to separate some image transformations and video encoding in a separate Process.
However, it works fine writing to video files, but fails when writing to a GStreamer pipeline.
Here's the result for:
* GStreamer pipeline, it hangs at `self.video_writer.write(frame)`. Note that it works just fine outside of a Process and I don't get any GStreamer warnings or errors:
```
Requested new frame
Writing frame to video writer...
 > Written frame!

Writing frame to video writer...

Requested new frame

```
* Video file, works file!

```
deprecated pixel format used, make sure you did set range correctly

Requested new frame
 > Written frame!
Requested new frame

Requested new frame
 > Written frame!
Requested new frame
Writing frame to video writer...
Requested new frame
 > Written frame!
Requested new frame
Writing frame to video writer...
Requested new frame
...
```
I'm not really sure on how I could debug this. Any suggestions are appreciated!
```python
import time
from multiprocessing import Event as ProcessEvent
from multiprocessing import Process, shared_memory

import cv2
import numpy as np
from picamera2 import MappedArray, Picamera2
from picamera2.request import CompletedRequest


class VideoEncoder(Process):
    def __init__(self):
        super(VideoEncoder, self).__init__()

        self.loop = ProcessEvent()
        self.loop.set()
        self.new_frame = ProcessEvent()
        self.new_frame.clear()

        # Doesn't work
        self.video_writer = cv2.VideoWriter(
            "my_very_long_gstreamer_pipeline",
            fourcc=0,
            fps=30,
            frameSize=(960, 720),
            isColor=True,
        )

        # # Works
        # self.video_writer = cv2.VideoWriter(
        #     "filename.avi",
        #     cv2.VideoWriter_fourcc(*"MJPG"),
        #     fps=30,
        #     frameSize=(960, 720),
        # )

        data = np.zeros((1232, 1640, 3), dtype=np.uint8).flatten()
        self.frame = shared_memory.SharedMemory(create=True, size=data.nbytes)

    def run(self):
        while self.loop.is_set():
            if self.new_frame.wait():
                self.process_frame()
                self.new_frame.clear()

        self.video_writer.release()

    def write(self, frame: np.ndarray):
        sha = np.ndarray(frame.shape, dtype=frame.dtype, buffer=self.frame.buf)
        sha[:] = frame[:]
        self.new_frame.set()
        print("Requested new frame")

    def process_frame(self):
        frame = np.ndarray((1232, 1640, 3), dtype=np.uint8, buffer=self.frame.buf)

        # Transform image in different ways...
        # Draw some stuff on image...
        # Takes some time...

        frame = cv2.resize(frame, (960, 720))

        print("Writing frame to video writer...")
        self.video_writer.write(frame)
        print(" > Written frame!")

    def stop(self):
        self.loop.clear()
        self.new_frame.set()


encoder = VideoEncoder()
encoder.start()


def cam_callback(request: CompletedRequest):
    with MappedArray(request, "main") as m:
        # Do some image processing...
        # Takes some time...

        # Once finished, send file to video stream
        encoder.write(m.array)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    picam = Picamera2(1)

    picam.configure(
        picam.create_video_configuration(
            main={"format": "RGB888", "size": (1640, 1232)}
        )
    )
    picam.pre_callback = cam_callback
    picam.start()

    time.sleep(10)

    picam.stop()
    encoder.stop()

```

title: Default Z-Axis For SolvePnP Charuco Board Seems to be Into Board - Inconsistent Indexing of Charuco Corners to Previous Versions
body:
**OpenCV Versions:**
Unexpected behavior in opencv-python==4.10.0 , expected behavior in opencv-contrib-python==4.5.5.62
****Operating System / Platform:** Ubuntu 22.04
Compiler and Compiler Version**: GCC 11.4.0
Python Version: 3.10.12
I'm working on a Charuco based camera calibration. My calibration previously worked in opencv-contrib-python==4.5.5.62 , and I was working on porting it to opencv-python=4.10.0 to get around the breaking changes introduced in 4.7.
However, I noticed inconsistencies between the order of corners returned by the the charuco detector, that lead to a rotation of 180 degrees about the X axis for estimated board pose (with SolvePnp) between versions. While the earlier version's Z axis points out of the board, in the later version, it points into the board (from SolvePnp)
I noticed that the indexing of the internal corners returned by the new charuco detector (after 4.7) seems to start at the
top left corner. However, the indexing of the internal corners returned by the old charuco detector (prior to 4.7) seems to start at the bottom left corner.
In order for the Z axis to point out of the board towards the camera, which is what I'd expect according to convention, I found
that I needed to renumber the indexing of the internal corners to start at the bottom left corner for the newer version  to match the behavior of the older version when input to SolvePnp. I understand that 4.7 was a breaking change, and as such can change the behavior for later versions, but I'd expect the Z axis to still point out of the board, which doesn't happen with the default behavior that I observed.
I'm wondering here if I made a mistake with the way I am choosing the inputs for SolvePNP, or if this axis was flipped intentionally to be into board between versions, or if this is unintended behavior. I also tried changing the inputs for SolvePNP (using object points instead of chessboard corners for 3d, and using aruco tag corners instead of internal chessboard corners for 2d) for the newer version, but found that this resulted in the Z axis still being into the board.
### Prior to 4.7.0
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8100886f-de7f-47f3-b7d3-fd40059d524f)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/60c19661-f3a7-43a4-a347-2da398bbf89a)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/13d28119-b53b-4d6a-9138-8d352388d869)

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a6504442-9178-49e4-b0b3-92e79c51509d)
## After 4.7
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c62061dd-2dda-4539-ae1b-cc88d8520aa5)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3da6c6bb-1dbb-4ffa-8f8a-62e11471a05c)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/925d86dc-ed3e-450e-afbc-8080aeee7415)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4d963ac6-42fd-4102-a69f-b38c51cf5f60)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/af7f2c50-09f5-46f9-b0cf-5e7b0873ef3d)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c4a836cf-ed57-4992-b0e2-4110652729d1)
### After 4.7.0 with enforcing ascending IDS from bottom left corner (Ad Hoc Bug Fix for Consistency):
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8734385e-8928-432c-985c-0989df065123)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2ee5fc36-74b5-4c60-a226-9311dfbf8798)

Potentially Related Issues:
https://github.com/opencv/opencv_contrib/issues/2921 : axis seems to be into board here
https://github.com/opencv/opencv/issues/23152 : broken backwards compatibility
First, download the gist from https://gist.github.com/glvov-bdai/d3d1c9f03d3d5ca6e61af296342498ff
Then, to recreate the figures I linked above, run the following in separate terminals (close figure to get next figure)
## Prior to 4.7.0 | In one terminal, for the older version (Out of Board)
```
python3 -m venv ~/venvs/older_cv2
source ~/venvs/older_cv2/bin/activate
pip install numpy==1.26.4
pip install opencv-contrib-python==4.5.5.62
pip install matplotlib
python3 opencv_charuco_detection.py
```
## After 4.7 | In another terminal, for the newer version (Into Board)
```
python3 -m venv ~/venvs/newer_cv2
source ~/venvs/newer_cv2/bin/activate
pip install opencv-python==4.10.0.84
pip install matplotlib
python3 opencv_charuco_detection.py --legacy
```
## In another terminal, for the newer version (After 4.7.0 with enforcing ascending IDS from bottom left corner (Ad Hoc Bug Fix for Consistency) (Out of Board):
```
source ~/venvs/newer_cv2/bin/activate
python3 opencv_charuco_detection.py --legacy --enforce_ascending_ids
```

title: Python: Incorrect result in cv2.morphologyEx() with cv2.MORPH_CLOSE
body:
OpenCV:  3.2.0
System: Ubuntu 16.04
I have observed low pixel accuracy while using morphological operations.
## Example ##
By blue 0s are shown and by red 1s
Image:
![not_filled_contours](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/18720200/34779133-f4956086-f627-11e7-934d-da9008c3a9a1.PNG)
I performed:
```
contours = cv2.findContours(img, contours, cv2.RETR_EXTERNAL, cv2.CHAIN_APPROX_NONE)
```
After this I created an image by filling the contours found:
```
import numpy as np
filled_im = np.zeros(img.shape, np.uint8)
cv2.drawContours(filled_im,contours, -1, 1, -1)
```
I performed closing operation to the original image and computed the difference between the filled image:
```
res_img = (cv2.morphologyEx(img.copy(), cv2.MORPH_CLOSE,
            np.ones((4,4), np.uint8))) * filled_img
```
The resulting image was expected to be the same as the first image, as far as the outer bounds are considered. This is not the case:
![diff](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/18720200/34779827-f83237f8-f629-11e7-9c8e-6fc8ebeb2422.PNG)
The same result is observed when using sequentially `cv2.dilate()` and `cv2.erode()`
Also, when not performing any morphological operation, the result is as expected, so contours operations are not to blame.

title: RQDecomp3x3 C++ Signature Does not Take eulerAngles
body: The `RQDecomp3x3` C++ method does not take `eulerAngles`, whereas the c method does. (see: http://docs.opencv.org/3.0-beta/modules/calib3d/doc/camera_calibration_and_3d_reconstruction.html?highlight=decomposeprojectionmatrix#rqdecomp3x3)

title: The parameter patternSize behaves unexpected in findCirclesGrid()
body:
OpenCV python version: 4.7.0.72
Operating System / Platform: macOS 13.3.1
Python version: 3.10.10
According to the [documentation](https://docs.opencv.org/3.4/d9/d0c/group__calib3d.html#gad1205c4b803a21597c7d6035f5efd775), `patternSize` should be `(points_per_row, points_per_colum)`. However, it seems that only when I set `patternSize` as `(points_per_colum, points_per_row)` can make the return value `retval` of `findCirclesGrid()` become `True`.
Code:
```python
import cv2

img = cv2.imread(f'test.png')

patternSize = (7, 10)
retval, centers = cv2.findCirclesGrid(img, patternSize, cv2.CirclesGridFinderParameters_SYMMETRIC_GRID)
print(f'{patternSize=} {retval=}')

patternSize = (10, 7)
retval, centers = cv2.findCirclesGrid(img, patternSize, cv2.CirclesGridFinderParameters_SYMMETRIC_GRID)
print(f'{patternSize=} {retval=}')
```
Output:
```
patternSize=(7, 10) retval=False
patternSize=(10, 7) retval=True
```
Test Data:
![test](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/47266984/231060351-23263d76-710d-4b60-8cdd-7b4fdec76615.png)

title: Errors trying to implement custom layer for ONNX DNN
body:
OpenCV python version: 4.7.0
Operating System / Platform: Manjaro
Python version: 3.10
I'm trying to implement a layer from the ONNX runtime ( [https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/main/docs/Operators.md#TopK](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/main/docs/Operators.md#TopK) ) which is not implemented in OpenCV with this Python code:
```python
import numpy as np

class TopKLayer:
    def __init__(self, params, blobs):
        self.k = blobs[0][0][0]
        self.axis = params.get("axis", -1)
        self.largest = params.get("largest", 1)
        self.sorted = params.get("sorted", 1)

    def getMemoryShapes(self, inputs):
        output_shape = list(inputs[0])
        output_shape[self.axis] = self.k
        return [tuple(output_shape)]

    def forward(self, inputs):
        inputs_to_sort = -inputs if self.largest else inputs
        if self.sorted:
            indices_uncropped = np.argsort(inputs_to_sort, self.axis)
        else:
            indices_uncropped = np.argpartition(inputs_to_sort, self.k, self.axis)
        indices = indices_uncropped[(*self.axis % inputs.ndim * (slice(None),), slice(self.k))]
        values = np.take_along_axis(inputs, indices, self.axis)
        return [values, indices]
```
Then when I try to load an ONNX model with the layer the program fails.
```python
import cv2
from pathlib import Path

cv2.dnn_registerLayer("TopK", TopKLayer)

onnx_model_path = Path('path/to/model.onnx')
opencv_net = cv2.dnn.readNetFromONNX(str(onnx_model_path)) 
```
With the code above it fails with
```
cv2.error: OpenCV(4.7.0) /io/opencv/modules/dnn/src/onnx/onnx_importer.cpp:1073: error: (-2:Unspecified error) in function 'handleNode'
> Node [Add@ai.onnx]:(onnx_node!/Add_1) parse error: OpenCV(4.7.0) /io/opencv/modules/dnn/src/onnx/onnx_importer.cpp:638: error: (-215:Assertion failed) shapeIt != outShapes.end() in function 'addLayer'
```
but when I try to replace the return statement in the `getMemoryShapes` method with either (I think this should be the correct return type but I don't really know)
```python
return tuple(output_shape)
```
or
```python
return [[tuple(output_shape)]]
```
It fails with
```
cv2.error: OpenCV(4.7.0) /io/opencv/modules/dnn/src/onnx/onnx_importer.cpp:1073: error: (-2:Unspecified error) in function 'handleNode'
> Node [TopK@ai.onnx]:(onnx_node!/TopK) parse error: OpenCV(4.7.0) /io/opencv/modules/dnn/misc/python/pyopencv_dnn.hpp:147: error: (-215:Assertion failed) pyopencv_to_generic_vec(res, outputs, ArgInfo("", 0)) in function 'getMemoryShapes'
```
I don't get to see the failmsgs from [`pyopencv_to_generic_vec`](https://github.com/opencv/opencv/blob/8bd17163c79231a4afbbdf978864cd2d0c6b1f67/modules/python/src2/cv2_convert.hpp#L267) but I guess it was triggered by this
```C++
        if (!pyopencv_to(item_wrap.item, value[i], info))
        {
            failmsg("Can't parse '%s'. Sequence item with index %lu has a wrong type", info.name, i);
            return false;
        }
```
since the sequence protocol check above should be fine. I have no idea why though.
run the code above

```
# Eigen is pure header library, no need to run make for building.
RUN cmake .. && \
    make install

# Install Ceres

RUN git checkout ${CERES_VERSION}   
RUN cmake .. && \

# Install OpenCV
WORKDIR /app/3rdparty
RUN wget https://github.com/opencv/opencv/archive/${OPENCV_VERSION}.zip -O opencv.zip \

    && unzip opencv_contrib.zip \
    && rm -rf opencv_contrib.zip

WORKDIR /app/3rdparty/opencv-${OPENCV_VERSION}/build
# https://github.com/moby/buildkit/blob/master/frontend/dockerfile/docs/reference.md#run---mounttypecache
RUN --mount=type=cache,mode=0777,target=/tmp/opencv-cache/ cmake .. \
    # To Speed up rebuilds
    -DOPENCV_DOWNLOAD_PATH=/tmp/opencv-cache/ \
    -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
    -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/usr/local \
    -DCUDA_ARCH_BIN=86 \
    -DWITH_CUDA=ON \
    -DWITH_CUBLAS=1 \
    -DWITH_CUDNN=ON \
    -DOPENCV_DNN_CUDA=ON \
    -DOPENCV_BUILD_3RDPARTY_LIBS=ON \
    -DBUILD_NEW_PYTHON_SUPPORT=ON \
    -DBUILD_opencv_python3=ON \
    -DBUILD_JPEG=ON \
    -DPYTHON_DEFAULT_EXECUTABLE=$(which python3) \
    # Build shared libraries (.dll/.so) instead of static ones (.lib/.a)
    -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=ON \
    -DOPENCV_ENABLE_NONFREE=ON \
    # Disable Intentionally.
    -DBUILD_TESTS=OFF \
    -DBUILD_PERF_TESTS=OFF \
    -DBUILD_EXAMPLES=OFF \
    -DBUILD_opencv_apps=OFF \
    -DBUILD_JAVA=OFF \
    -DOPENCV_EXTRA_MODULES_PATH=/app/3rdparty/opencv_contrib-${OPENCV_VERSION}/modules && \
    make -j$(nproc) && \
    make install
```
a. Install `libjpeg-dev` and build opencv  version `3.4.19` _without_ `-DBUILD_JPEG=ON` flag. This fails to write Jpeg files or read jpeg file.
b. Install `libjpeg-dev` and build opencv  version `4.7.0` _without_ `-DBUILD_JPEG=ON` flag. This fails to write Jpeg files or read jpeg file.
c. Install `libjpeg-turbo8-dev` and build opencv  version `4.7.0` _without_ `-DBUILD_JPEG=ON` flag. This fails to write Jpeg files or read jpeg file.
d. Install `libjpeg-turbo8-dev` and build opencv  version `4.7.0` _with_ `-DBUILD_JPEG=ON` flag. This reads and writes jpeg file correctly.
```cpp
cv::Mat colorDummyImage(480, 640, CV_8UC3, cv::Scalar(0, 0, 255));
std::cout << cv::getVersionString() << " - Writing jpeg" << std::endl;
cv::imwrite("1.jpg", colorDummyImage);  // Crashes with segmentation fault.
std::cout << "Write done" << std::endl;
colorDummyImage = cv::imread("1.jpg", -1);
std::cout << colorDummyImage.empty() << std::endl;  // This code returns an empty image if I give path to a real jpeg file.
// Tested file and its validity using command `file /app/lhindscan/bin/data_2023-02-28_18-57-28_slope-4400/image/00038.jpg`, prints proper 640x480 dimension and jpeg format.
```
Just in case this might be a disk permission issue, I had changed extension from jpg to png and both read and write worked fine and ofcourse on adding `-DBUILD_JPEG=ON` flag for building opencv, the issue went away for jpeg.
Also, went through multiple stackoverflow and github issues but didnt find anything.

title: Make proper layers implementations to support the new dnn engine in 5.x
body:
See https://github.com/opencv/opencv/pull/26056
The layers are the following:
the new implementations must:
1. conform with ONNX specification (https://onnx.ai/onnx/operators/index.html), different opset's
2. not rely on the shape inference being done in ONNX importer
3. preferably, be 'stateless'. In particular, forget about Layer::finalize(). Don't rely on it, leave it empty.
4. support different data types. All the layers that just move tensor elements around without arithmetic operations on them may just have branches for 1-byte, 2-byte, 4-byte and 8-byte elements, then they will support all possible data types, except for the recently added to ONNX specs int4/uint4/float4, which OpenCV does not support anyway (yet)

```
def classify_frame(net, inputQueue, outputQueue):
	# keep looping

		if not inputQueue.empty():

			# set the blob as input to our deep learning object

	help="path to Caffe 'deploy' prototxt file")
ap.add_argument("-m", "--model", required=True,
	help="path to Caffe pre-trained model")
ap.add_argument("-c", "--confidence", type=float, default=0.2,
	help="minimum probability to filter weak detections")
args = vars(ap.parse_args())

# initialize the list of class labels MobileNet SSD was trained to
# detect, then generate a set of bounding box colors for each class
CLASSES = ["background", "aeroplane", "bicycle", "bird", "boat",
	"bottle", "bus", "car", "cat", "chair", "cow", "diningtable",
	"dog", "horse", "motorbike", "person", "pottedplant", "sheep",
	"sofa", "train", "tvmonitor"]
COLORS = np.random.uniform(0, 255, size=(len(CLASSES), 3))

# load our serialized model from disk
print("[INFO] loading model...")
#net = cv2.dnn.readNetFromCaffe(args["prototxt"], args["model"])
net = cv2.imread(args["model"],cv2.IMREAD_GRAYSCALE)

# initialize the input queue (frames), output queue (detections),
# and the list of actual detections returned by the child process
inputQueue = Queue(maxsize=1)
outputQueue = Queue(maxsize=1)
detections = None

# construct a child process *indepedent* from our main process of
# execution
print("[INFO] starting process...")
p = Process(target=classify_frame, args=(net, inputQueue,
	outputQueue,))
p.daemon = True
p.start()

# initialize the video stream, allow the cammera sensor to warmup,
# and initialize the FPS counter
print("[INFO] starting video stream...")
vs = capture()
# vs = VideoStream(usePiCamera=True).start()
time.sleep(2.0)
fps = FPS().start()
thresh = cv2.getTrackbarPos('thresh','Lepton')
# loop over the frames from the video stream
while True:
	# grab the frame from the threaded video stream, resize it, and
	# grab its imensions
	frame = vs
	frame = imutils.resize(frame, width=400)
	(fH, fW) = frame.shape[:2]

	# if the input queue *is* empty, give the current frame to
	# classify
	if inputQueue.empty():
		inputQueue.put(frame)

	# if the output queue *is not* empty, grab the detections
	if not outputQueue.empty():
		detections = outputQueue.get()

	# check to see if our detectios are not None (and if so, we'll
	# draw the detections on the frame)
	if detections is not None:
		# loop over the detections
		for i in np.arange(0, detections.shape[2]):
			# extract the confidence (i.e., probability) associated
			# with the prediction
			confidence = detections[0, 0, i, 2]

			# filter out weak detections by ensuring the `confidence`
			# is greater than the minimum confidence
			if confidence < args["confidence"]:
				continue

			# otherwise, extract the index of the class label from
			# the `detections`, then compute the (x, y)-coordinates
			# of the bounding box for the object
			idx = int(detections[0, 0, i, 1])
			dims = np.array([fW, fH, fW, fH])
			box = detections[0, 0, i, 3:7] * dims
			(startX, startY, endX, endY) = box.astype("int")
			_ ,frame = cv2.threshold(img,thresh,255,cv2.THRESH_TOZERO)
			frame = cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_GRAY2BGR);
			frame = applyCustomColorMap(frame);
			# draw the prediction on the frame
			label = "{}: {:.2f}%".format(CLASSES[idx],
				confidence * 100)
			cv2.rectangle(frame, (startX, startY), (endX, endY),
				COLORS[idx], 2)
			y = startY - 15 if startY - 15 > 15 else startY + 15
			cv2.putText(frame, label, (startX, y),
				cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.5, COLORS[idx], 2)

	# show the output frame
	cv2.imshow("Frame", frame)
	key = cv2.waitKey(1) & 0xFF

	# if the `q` key was pressed, break from the loop
	if key == ord("q"):
		break

	# update the FPS counter
	fps.update()

# stop the timer and display FPS information
fps.stop()
print("[INFO] elapsed time: {:.2f}".format(fps.elapsed()))
print("[INFO] approx. FPS: {:.2f}".format(fps.fps()))

# do a bit of cleanup
cv2.destroyAllWindows()
vs.stop()
```
</details>

title: Segmentation fault on ParallelLoopBodyWrapperContext::finalize with ENABLE_INSTRUMENTATION=ON
body:
OpenCV version: 4.9.0
Platform: Nvidia Jetson Orin Developer Kit
Operating System: JetPack 5.1.2 - Ubuntu 20.04
Compiler & compiler version: GCC 9.4.0
Compilation line:
```bash
cmake -DCMAKE_VERBOSE_MAKEFILE=ON -DOPENCV_EXTRA_MODULES_PATH="$cv_contrib_pkg_path/modules"  \
-DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DBUILD_WITH_DEBUG_INFO=ON \
-DENABLE_PROFILING=ON -DENABLE_INSTRUMENTATION=ON \
-DOPENCV_ENABLE_MEMORY_SANITIZER=ON -DENABLE_BUILD_HARDENING=ON \
-DOPENCV_ENABLE_NONFREE=ON -DOPENCV_GENERATE_PKGCONFIG=ON -DOPENCV_LAPACK_FIND_PACKAGE_ONLY=OFF \
-DOpenGL_GL_PREFERENCE=GLVND -DINSTALL_TO_MANGLED_PATHS=ON -DWITH_EIGEN=ON -DWITH_LAPACK=ON \
-DWITH_CUDA=ON -DWITH_CUFFT=ON -DWITH_CUBLAS=ON -DWITH_CUDNN=ON -DWITH_VA=ON -DWITH_OPENCL=ON \
-DWITH_OPENCL_SVM=ON -DWITH_TBB=ON -DWITH_OPENMP=ON -DWITH_ITT=ON -DWITH_IMGCODEC_PXM=ON \
-DWITH_IMGCODEC_PFM=ON -DWITH_JPEG=ON -DWITH_PNG=ON -DWITH_TIFF=ON -DWITH_OPENEXR=ON \
-DWITH_GDAL=ON -DWITH_GDCM=ON -DWITH_JASPER=OFF -DWITH_V4L=ON -DWITH_FFMPEG=ON \
-DWITH_GSTREAMER=ON -DWITH_1394=ON -DWITH_PVAPI=OFF -DWITH_XINE=OFF -DWITH_GTK=ON -DWITH_VTK=ON \
-DWITH_QT=OFF -DWITH_FREETYPE=ON -DWITH_PROTOBUF=ON -DOPENCV_DNN_OPENCL=ON -DOPENCV_DNN_CUDA=ON \
-DWITH_VULKAN=ON -DWITH_TESSERACT=ON -DWITH_QUIRC=ON -DWITH_ADE=ON -DWITH_NGRAPH=OFF \
-DWITH_CLP=ON -DBUILD_opencv_python2=ON -DBUILD_opencv_python3=ON -DBUILD_opencv_dnn=ON \
-DBUILD_opencv_dnn_modern=ON -DBUILD_opencv_face=ON -DBUILD_JAVA=OFF -DBUILD_PROTOBUF=ON \
-DBUILD_EXAMPLES=OFF -DBUILD_PERF_TESTS=OFF -DBUILD_TBB=ON -DBUILD_ITT=ON -DWITH_ARAVIS=ON \
-DWITH_UEYE=ON -DWITH_OPENNI2=ON -DWITH_LIBREALSENSE=ON -DWITH_CAROTENE=ON -DWITH_NVCUVID=OFF \
-DWITH_NVCUVENC=OFF -DWITH_OPENCLAMDFFT=OFF -DWITH_OPENCLAMDBLAS=OFF -DWITH_TENGINE=ON \
-DWITH_FLATBUFFERS=ON -DOPENCV_DNN_TFLITE=ON -S "$cv_pkg_path"
```
A segmentation fault is thrown when the code uses a `parallel_for`.
First discovered debayering an image, and after, also appeared trying to rectify an image.
Looking at the backtrace shows that the error was [here](https://github.com/opencv/opencv/blob/4.9.0/modules/core/src/parallel.cpp#L227).
This made me suspicious about the `#ifdef ENABLE_INSTRUMENTATION`, witch I recently enabled on the compilation.
Using this compilation have also appeared runtime errors regarding the TLS which I previously managed by setting `LD_PRELOAD=libgomp.so.1` enviroment variable.
All of this made me try to disable the instrumentation, which has worked to remove the error for now.
In this case the code that ran the OpenCV instructions is from [here](https://github.com/ros-perception/image_pipeline/blob/noetic/image_proc/src/nodelets/debayer.cpp#L235) and [here](https://github.com/ros-perception/vision_opencv/blob/noetic/image_geometry/src/pinhole_camera_model.cpp#L541).
But a minimal example would be just:
```cpp
cv::Mat bayered_img, bgr_img;

bayered_img = <capture_bayered_image_from_camera>;

cv::cvtColor(bayered_img, bgr_img, cv::COLOR_BayerBG2BGR);
```

title: Question about deploying ONNX model using OpenCV DNN and CUDA
body:
### Question
**Description:**
When deploying a YOLOv8 model using ONNX with OpenCV's dnn module, I encountered an issue where the model outputs bounding boxes with all coordinates set to zero when using `cv::dnn::DNN_BACKEND_CUDA` and `cv::dnn::DNN_TARGET_CUDA`. However, the class confidences are correct. When I switch to CPU (not using CUDA), the model produces correct bounding boxes and class confidences.
The issue does not occur with YOLOv5 under the same deployment conditions (CUDA backend).
The ONNX model was exported using the standard export process for YOLOv8.
**Additional**
My environment is CUDA 10.2 + OpenCV 4.8 with CUDA.
Does the CUDA backend in OpenCV 4.8 not support certain ONNX operators used in YOLOv8?

title: cv::norm(...,...,cv::NORM_L2SQR) is 10x slower than calling cv::normL2Sqr(...)
body:
Used Version: OpenCV 4.5.4 and OpenCV 4.6.0
Calling cv::norm is 10x slower than calling cv::normL2Sqr directly.
I tested measured the time with 2.5 Million float vectors with 512 dimensions.
cv::normL2Sqr:     308ms
cv::norm:            3092ms
Im wondering how there can be such an performance loss.
There is also no avx optimization in opencv for cv::norm, but i found some implementations:
https://github.com/anas-899/l2_distance_SIMD/blob/master/Source.cpp
https://gist.github.com/matsui528/583925f88fcb08240319030202588c74
Maybe somebody with avx knowledge wants to take this over?

title: Support audio in OpenCV
body:
Hello!
At the moment, OpenCV library and deep learning do not stand still.
Already more often systems are observed where audio and video are simultaneously used for processing.
Therefore, question arose of creating a `audioio` module.
Plan of integrate `audioio` module:
To try support ALSA (Advanced Linux Sound Architecture):
* Read/Write audio files (for example `wav`)
* Support audio input/output stream (support standard devices)
Your comments are welcome!

title: KDTree Index doesn't handle floating point NaN value
body:
OpenCV Version: 4.7.0
Operating System: macOS Darwin 22.4.0 Darwin Kernel Version 22.4.0: Mon Mar  6 20:59:28 PST 2023; root:xnu-8796.101.5~3/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000 arm64
Python version: Python 3.11.3
When input descriptors contain `NaN` (or `inf`, or very large floating point number) can cause the KDTree index to break while BFMatcher is fine.
## KDTree Index
```
>>> matcher
< cv2.FlannBasedMatcher 0x10421f0f0>
>>> des1
array([[ 1.,  2.,  3.],
       [nan, nan, nan]], dtype=float32)
>>> des2
array([[1., 2., 3.],
       [4., 5., 6.]], dtype=float32)
>>> matcher.knnMatch(des1, des2, k=2)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
cv2.error: OpenCV(4.7.0) /tmp/opencv-20230423-16173-kbjcfg/opencv-4.7.0/modules/flann/include/opencv2/flann/kdtree_index.h:468: error: (-215:Assertion failed) result.full() in function 'getNeighbors'
```
## BFMatcher
```
>>> matcher2 = cv2.BFMatcher()
>>> matcher2.knnMatch(des1, des2, k=2)
((< cv2.DMatch 0x1449dc190>, < cv2.DMatch 0x1449dc250>), ())
>>>
```
Seems like the error comes from [this Assert](https://github.com/opencv/opencv/blob/0052d46b8e33c7bfe0e1450e4bff28b88f455570/modules/flann/include/opencv2/flann/kdtree_index.h#L468)
And it's caused by [this line](https://github.com/opencv/opencv/blob/0052d46b8e33c7bfe0e1450e4bff28b88f455570/modules/flann/include/opencv2/flann/result_set.h#L192) in the `ResultSet` floating point NaN comparison is problematic.
See Description.

title: OpenCV core shared library load speed is extremely slow
body:
Operating System / Platform: Ubuntu 20.04
Operating System / Platform: Windows 10
Operating System / Platform: MacOS Monterey
Have tried all sorts of OS.
Have tried both pre built versions of OpenCV and building myself.
Have tried release and non optimized builds.
Simply loading the OpenCV core library is slow in all programming languages.
Even though the dll/dylib is only 3MB, it takes as much as 200ms to load it.
This is very unusual as other libraries that are over 10MB take 0-1ms.
This load speed is very important for use case such in mobile apps.
I understand OpenCV is feature complex, so, I have isolated it to just the core. Many uses of OpenCV only involve resizing of images, so it would be beneficial to understand why simply loading it takes so long.
I have attached an example dylib that was compiled on my M1 Macbook.
[libopencv_core.4.9.0.dylib.zip](https://github.com/opencv/opencv/files/13854941/libopencv_core.4.9.0.dylib.zip)
```java
System.load("path_to_dll_or_dylib");
```
You can load it any way you like in any programming language. It will be the same.

title: VIDEOIO ERROR: V4L2: Pixel format unsupported (Philips SPC 900NC)
body:
##### System information (version)
- OpenCV => 3.2.0
- Operating System / Platform => Linux / Kubuntu 16.04 64 bit
- Compiler => g++
My opencv installation works fine with most webcams, except for a **Philips SPC 900NC** CCD webcam.
From searching the Internet I gather that there was a time when opencv supported this camera, but it seems that something changed since then, probably after _v4l_ changed to _v4l2_. This link, http://nuigroup.com/forums/viewthread/291/ ,  has a patch to make the camera work in _YUV420P_ mode, but it looks like most of this code is already included in the current release. A number of multimedia and communications programs, e.g. Cheese, VLC, Skype and others, can access the camera without a problem in Linux, but e.g. mjpg-streamer can not.
##### How to reproduce the problem:
Compile a demo program, e.g. _bg_sub.cpp_ from the tutorials, and run it with this camera:
```
$ ./bg_sub -vid /dev/video0
Welcome! OpenCV version: 3.2.0 (3.2.0)
VIDEOIO ERROR: V4L2: Pixel format of incoming image is unsupported by OpenCV
Could not initialize capturing. Exiting...
```
I can provide more debug info about the VIDEOIO ERROR, pixel format, etc, if someone can suggest a way to do it properly.

Can I modify the order of images in matching stage so that I get a particular scene in the centre of my panorama?
Do I need to reorder my feature vector before providing it to matcher?
Example 360 degree image:
Can I recenter the “river” to middle?

OpenCV: FFMPEG: fallback to use tag 0x7634706d/'mp4v'

title: Rotate Parameter for putText() function
body:
I am trying to rotate my putText() without rotating the whole frame. But it seems like a hard thing to do. Is it possible to add a new "Rotate" parameter to putText() function?
TIA

title: [Android] matchShapes is not Rotational Invariant - possible bug with HuMoments?
body:
Can somebody verify that the matchShapes function is rotationally invariant in OpenCV 3.0 Android?
I'm currently testing the function and I only get low values for matchShapes (I'm using method Imgproc.CV_CONTOURS_MATCH_I1) when I rotate the target contour to 45 or 135 degrees. When the target contour is at 90 or 180, I get a very high value for the result. I'm using MatOfPoint for both contours, and I'm using identical code to what I have already working on iOS and Python.
```
            double matchPercentage = Imgproc.matchShapes(contours.get(i), logoContour, Imgproc.CV_CONTOURS_MATCH_I1, 0);
```
Possibly a bug with HuMoments?

title: Feature request to use Intel Myriad chips for acceleration Depth Map from Stereo Images
body:
Feature request to use Intel Myriad chips for acceleration Depth Map from Stereo Images by using Myriad SHAVES cores - to accelerate OpenCV functions: https://docs.opencv.org/4.1.1/dd/d53/tutorial_py_depthmap.html
Currently, OpenCV can use Myriad X chips for Deep Learning tasks (via OpenVINO DL IE backend), but not for other tasks like Stereo Depth processing.
As known KEY BENEFITS of Myriad X chip is: **New Vision Accelerators including Stereo Depth** https://www.movidius.com/MyriadX But there are no examples or API for Stereo Depth processing by using OpenVINO in free access.
It would be nice to be able to use uniform OpenCV API for the basic functions of the Miriad X chip in conjunction with a very weak CPU (Intel Atom). Because Atom CPU doesn't have enough performance to get a Stereo-Depth-Map on its own with high FPS.

title: How to get 2D image correspondances for 3D CAD model points for non textured objects?
body:
Hi
I have to estimate a very accurate 6D Pose of regular shape objects (like blocks for construction ) without any textured. I have the 3D CAD model of the object, and I know roughly the coordinates of the blocks. So the camera is pointing down to the object and can use stereo cameras or ZED.
I know roughly the coordinates on the object and the camera will be placed around 1m away from the object. I would like to use OpenCV solvePnP method and for that need to find the 2d correspondences of the scene image for 3D CAD model points of the objects.
I search and found this tutorial for [https://docs.opencv.org/master/dc/d2c/tutorial_real_time_pose.html](url)  but in my case the objects are nontextured. So I can not use such key descriptors like SURF or SIFT or ORB. And also I would like to use it in real-time and as input to take my camera input.
Hence, research in this area has focused on tracking using natural features, such as edge-based, optical flow-based,template-based, and keypoint-based. Is this relevant so I can find the 2d correspondences for 3d cad model points for non-textured objects like construction blocks and can get the 6D Pose?
Also, I know that exists Harris Corners, Hough Lines, Canny Edge, or Fast Corners descriptors but not sure if using them can track the object and find the 2D-3D points correspondences. Can I use them in [https://docs.opencv.org/master/dc/d2c/tutorial_real_time_pose.html](url) and modify the source code for real-time so I can track my non-textured objects? If yes what should I change in the source code and how to run it in real-time?
Any help would appreciate.

title: While cross-validation one or more of the classes have "   "been fell out of the sample. Try to reduce <Params::k_fold>
Allow exception-less compilation
body:
### Descripe the feature and motivation
Because of try/catch/throw, OpenCV cannot be compiled with exceptions disabled (with `-fno-exception` on clang). Compiling with exceptions creates significant binary-sized overhead (and sometimes even compilation and CPU overhead).  For embedded systems, having a binary as small as possible is needed. Google also does not use exceptions: https://google.github.io/styleguide/cppguide.html#Exceptions
In https://github.com/opencv/opencv/commit/7349b8f5cecfc6eb649b224e58da16d137c3c578, macros `CV_TRY/CV_CATCH` were introduced to use `try/catch` with exceptions and `abort` without, especially for embedded systems.
Those macros are still in 3.x but not in 4.x: https://github.com/opencv/opencv/blob/aba2167d9cc7dd7ff21cbca48a7713e55500da1a/modules/core/include/opencv2/core/cvdef.h#L176
Could they be brought back to 4.x/5.x ? I understand some OpenCV code relies on third party code and therefore exceptions (e.g. imgcodecs/videoio/dnn) but if most code can be compiled without exceptions, it will already be a win. I'll gladly make a pull request to switch most of the code to `CV_TRY/CV_CATCH` if they are back.

body:
OpenCV python version: 4.7.0.72
Operating System / Platform: Windows 10
Python version: 3.10.6
I am trying to run an int8 quantized model exported to ONNX with opencv backend.
The **problem** is with the output of the model. Running inference on **different** images gives the **same** output tensor.
I tried the inference with pytorch (quantized .pth model) and with openvino (same onnx file) and they give good results.
OpenCV output:
![opencv_output_tensor](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/69692905/223679691-bfb8bb75-c454-447c-9c6b-376802e656bf.PNG)
OpenVino output:
![openvino_out_tensor](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/69692905/223679826-d499c60c-bfbe-4c96-916e-928a8e8e6ae4.PNG)
Note: This is not a fully trained model, so it should not return the correct label!
The model:
[int8_quantized.zip](https://github.com/opencv/opencv/files/10919343/int8_quantized.zip)
Test images:
[test.zip](https://github.com/opencv/opencv/files/10919466/test.zip)
```
import openvino.runtime as vino
import cv2
import argparse
import numpy as np
import random
import time

def postprocess(output_blob,_labels):
        _labels=['beige', 'black', 'blue', 'brown', 'golden', 'green', 'grey', 'red', 'silver', 'white']
        batched_class_id_list = []
        for o in output_blob:
            #o=softmax(o)
            class_id_list = o.argsort()[::-1][:1]
            batched_class_id_list.append(class_id_list)
        if len(_labels) > 0:
            batched_predicted_labels = []
            for class_id_list in batched_class_id_list:
                predicted_labels = []
                for class_id in class_id_list:
                    predicted_labels.append(_labels[class_id])
                batched_predicted_labels.append(predicted_labels)
            return batched_predicted_labels
        else:
            return batched_class_id_list

ie = vino.Core()
model = ie.read_model(r"model_path.onnx")
cv_model = cv2.dnn.readNet(r"model_path.onnx")

compiled_model = ie.compile_model(model=model, device_name="CPU")
input_layer = compiled_model.input(0)
img_size = (112, 112) 

src_img = cv2.imread(r"img_path.jpg")
src_size = src_img.shape[:2]

img = cv2.resize(src_img,img_size, interpolation=cv2.INTER_LINEAR)
img = cv2.cvtColor(img, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)
img = img.astype(dtype=np.float32)
img /= 255.0
input_image = np.expand_dims(img.transpose(2, 0, 1), 0)
cv_model.setInput(input_image)
cv_outputs = cv_model.forward()
cv_pred=postprocess(cv_outputs,None)
pred = compiled_model([input_image])

print("OPENCV OUTPUT TENSOR:")
print(cv_outputs)
print("OPENVINO OUTPUT TENSOR:")
print(pred)
tmp_out=pred[compiled_model.output(0)]

#print(postprocess(tmp_out,None))



```

title: Invalid Depth order causing assertion to fail
body:
OpenCV version: 4.5.0
Operating System / Platform: Windows 10
Compiler & compiler version: GCC 9.3.0
When trying to save a matrix that has the depth CV_16F using cv::FileStorage the assertion `depth >=0 && depth <= CV_64F in function 'cv::fs::typeSymbol'` fails.
A possible explanation of the error might be found in `opencv2/core/hal/interface.h`. The order of the different depth definitions is incorrect and therefore the assertion fails:
```cpp
#define CV_8U   0
#define CV_8S   1
#define CV_16U  2
#define CV_16S  3
#define CV_32S  4
#define CV_32F  5
#define CV_64F  6
#define CV_16F  7
```
Therefore `CV_64F <= CV_16F` and if `CV_16F = depth` then `depth <= CV_64F` is `false` and the assertion failed. I did not manage to update opencv to 4.7.0 using vcpkg and therefore I could not verify if the issue still persists beyond 4.5.0.
```cpp
cv::Mat test = cv::Mat::zeros({100, 100}, CV_16F);
cv::FileStorage file("test.yml", cv::FileStorage::WRITE);
file.write("Test Mat", test);
```

title: OpenCVTutorial2MixedProcessing project compiled static library can not find the required function
body:
Hello!
I debug the openCVTutorial2MixedProcessing project, the use of the NDK compiler static library libmixed_sample.so, running APK in the 184 line of JavaCameraView error, showing the lack of n_Mat (int rows, int cols, int type).
I see through the IDA libmixed_sample.so confirmed this point, does not have this function, see libopencv_java3.so when can find this function, so I continue to view the libopencv_core.a that also can not find the function, this is the reason why libmixed_sample.so can't find.
I would like to compile static library how to get this function, so that the smooth operation of openCVTutorial2MixedProcessing?
I also have been identified successfully loaded System.loadLibrary ("mixed_sample");, just loaded this a dynamic library, and no load java3, this is what I want, because java3 8.5M, while libmixed_sample.so is only 2.3M, which can save a lot of APK in loading multiple platform dynamic library volume.
————————————————————————
您好！
我在调试openCVTutorial2MixedProcessing工程，使用了ndk编译静态库libmixed_sample.so，运行APK时在JavaCameraView的184行报错，显示缺少了n_Mat(int rows, int cols, int type)。
我通过IDA查看libmixed_sample.so证实了这一点，确实没有此函数，查看libopencv_java3.so时候却能够发现此函数，于是我继续查看libopencv_core.a发现也找不到此函数，这就是libmixed_sample.so找不到的原因。
请问我编译静态库该怎样才能得到此函数，让openCVTutorial2MixedProcessing顺利运行？
另外我确定已经成功加载了System.loadLibrary("mixed_sample");，只是加载了这一个动态库，并没有加载java3，这是我想要的，因为java3有8.5M，而libmixed_sample.so只有2.3M，这在加载多个平台动态库时能够节省很多APK的体积。

title: undefined reference to cv::dnn::blobFromImage().
body: I am trying to blobFromImage() from cv::dnn module and i am facing undefined reference to cv::dnn::blobFromImage(), please suggest me library I need to link for this function.

title: Python Scalar is different with C++
body:
Hi,
I suspect the following code has different behavior in C++ and Python.
```py
Scalar a = 1.0;
```
For Python, the `Scalar a = 1.0` code will only replace the first `0.0` to `1.0`.
Do something like `a[0] = 1.0`.
For C++, it will do the `Scalar a = Scalar(1.0)`.
Original link: https://forum.opencv.org/t/image2blobparams-c-and-python/13437?u=zihao_mu
My code in python :
```py
paramTF2cedn = cv.dnn.Image2BlobParams()
paramTF2cedn.datalayout = cv.dnn.DNN_LAYOUT_NHWC;
paramTF2cedn.ddepth = cv.CV_32F;
paramTF2cedn.mean = (128, 128, 128)
paramTF2cedn.scalefactor = 1 / 128.
paramTF2cedn.size = (224, 224)
paramTF2cedn.swapRB = False;
paramTF2cedn.paddingmode = cv.dnn.DNN_PMODE_NULL
print("paramTF2cedn.scalefactor = ", paramTF2cedn.scalefactor)
```
result is
```paramTF2cedn.scalefactor = (0.0078125, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0)```
in C++
```cpp
Image2BlobParams paramTF2cedn;
paramTF2cedn.datalayout = DNN_LAYOUT_NHWC;
paramTF2cedn.ddepth = CV_32F;
paramTF2cedn.mean = (128, 128, 128);
paramTF2cedn.scalefactor = 1 / 128.;
paramTF2cedn.size = Size(224, 224);
paramTF2cedn.swapRB = false;
paramTF2cedn.paddingmode = DNN_PMODE_NULL;
cout << "paramTF2cedn.scalefactor = " << paramTF2cedn.scalefactor << endl;
```
result is
```paramTF2cedn.scalefactor = [0.0078125, 0, 0, 0]```
It is about opencv-python bing.
install lastest opencv-python
`pip install opencv-python-rolling==4.7.0.20230603`.
```py
param = cv.dnn.Image2BlobParams()
param.mean = np.array([0.2, 0.3, 0.4]) # [0.2, 0.3, 0.4, 0.0]
# then redefine it.
param.mean = 1.0 # it is [1.0, 0.3, 0.4, 0.0]  <<--
```

body: my 1 usb 2 camera stereo cam cant change resolution over 320p on both cameras , however i can make my first cap up to 320 and second up to 720 but i cant make it other way around , second always has to be higher and if i wanna use both at same resolution im stuck with 320-320 . i get src empty when i try to show image thats taken when these conditions are not met .

title: Can not create contours using numpy array
body:
OpenCV python version: 4.7
Operating System / Platform: Ubuntu 18.04
Python version: 3.8.13
I have lat long point, so I want to make it to contours, to use some advanced function in cv2: [DisTransform](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/53646022/opencv-c-find-inscribing-circle-of-a-contour) to calculate the radius, but cant not created
Here is my data:
```
[[105.81407745  21.00965477]
 [105.81404398  21.00963977]
 [105.81403749  21.00963667]
 [105.81402883  21.00965337]
 [105.81405673  21.00966633]
 [105.81408515  21.00967953]]

```
I want to check by using draw contours:
```
cv2.error: OpenCV(4.7.0) /io/opencv/modules/imgproc/src/drawing.cpp:2508: error: (-215:Assertion failed) npoints > 0 in function 'drawContours'
```
```
    cnts = np.array([[105.81407745,  21.00965477]
                    [105.81404398,  21.00963977]
                    [105.81403749,  21.00963667]
                    [105.81402883,  21.00965337]
                    [105.81405673,  21.00966633]
                    [105.81408515,  21.00967953]])


    drawing = np.zeros([100, 100], np.uint8)
    cnts = [cnts]
    cv2.drawContours(drawing,cnts,-1,(255,255,255),2)
    cv2.imshow("Test", drawing)
    cv2.waitKey()
```

title: copyMakeBorder causes memory leak
body:
Hi, I'm pretty sure copyMakeBorder causes memory leaks in Python.
My workflow is pretty simple: I iterate over a folder with images. For each image, I apply `copyMakeBorder`, make some computations and then explicitely `del` the result. `tracemalloc` indicates that the call to `copyMakeBorder` creates a lot of memory that is never freed.

title: Compatibility of Cmake find_package for opencv 2 and 3
body:
For a project with opencv as its dependency, how to make it compatible to both opencv 2 and 3?
Since some basic functions are moved to the imgcodes module in opencv 3, the `find_package()` is different for version 2 and 3.
```
find_package(OpenCV COMPONENTS core highgui imgproc)  # if opencv 2 is installed
find_package(OpenCV COMPONENTS core highgui imgproc imgcodecs)  # if opencv 3 is installed
```
Any suggestions how to write the `CMakeLists.txt` to make it work with both 2 and 3 without manually specifying any path or version number?

title: GTK3 getWindowProperty for  cv::WND_PROP_VISIBLE  not implemented
Traincascade: grabbing negative window samples is seriously broken
body:
### Please state the information for your system
- OpenCV version: 3.x --> latest master branch
- Host OS: Linux (Ubuntu 14.04)
### In which part of the OpenCV library you got the issue?
apps --> opencv_traincascade process
### Expected behaviour
Providing more then enough negative samples is a common approach in computer vision. This makes sure that through the rescaling and subsampling windows, the train cascade application succeeds in grabbing multiple millions of windows from a set of larger images.
However this is not the case IF you supply a limited set of data.
### Actual behaviour & problem description
Imagine the following setup (just confirmed on a detector setup I have here)
- Given 2500 positive windows of 19x14 pixels
- Given 1 negative image of 66x28 pixels, from which negative windows can be grabbed
- Training LBP feature cascade classifier with `-numStages 25 -w 19 -h 14`
### Generated output
```
spu@TOBCAT:/data/zonnepanelen_test$ opencv_traincascade -data cascade/ -vec data.vec -bg negatives.txt -numPos 2500 -numNeg 10000 -numStages 25 -precalcValBufSize 8000 -precalcIdxBufSize 8000 -w 19 -h 14 -featureType LBP -minHitRate 0.999
PARAMETERS:
cascadeDirName: cascade/
vecFileName: data.vec
bgFileName: negatives.txt
numPos: 2500
numNeg: 10000
numStages: 25
precalcValBufSize[Mb] : 8000
precalcIdxBufSize[Mb] : 8000
acceptanceRatioBreakValue : -1
stageType: BOOST
featureType: LBP
sampleWidth: 19
sampleHeight: 14
boostType: GAB
minHitRate: 0.999
maxFalseAlarmRate: 0.5
weightTrimRate: 0.95
maxDepth: 1
maxWeakCount: 100
Number of unique features given windowSize [19,14] : 1710

===== TRAINING 0-stage =====
<BEGIN
POS count : consumed   2500 : 2500
NEG count : acceptanceRatio    10000 : 1
Precalculation time: 0
+----+---------+---------+
|  N |    HR   |    FA   |
+----+---------+---------+
|   1|        1|        1|
+----+---------+---------+
|   2|        1|        1|
+----+---------+---------+
|   3|        1|        1|
+----+---------+---------+
|   4|   0.9996|   0.1268|
+----+---------+---------+
END>
Training until now has taken 0 days 0 hours 0 minutes 3 seconds.

===== TRAINING 1-stage =====
<BEGIN
POS count : consumed   2500 : 2501
NEG count : acceptanceRatio    10000 : 0.127781
Precalculation time: 0
+----+---------+---------+
|  N |    HR   |    FA   |
+----+---------+---------+
|   1|        1|        1|
+----+---------+---------+
|   2|        1|        1|
+----+---------+---------+
|   3|        1|        1|
+----+---------+---------+
|   4|   0.9996|   0.0994|
+----+---------+---------+
END>
Training until now has taken 0 days 0 hours 0 minutes 8 seconds.

===== TRAINING 2-stage =====
<BEGIN
POS count : consumed   2500 : 2502
NEG count : acceptanceRatio    10000 : 0.0126893
Precalculation time: 0
+----+---------+---------+
|  N |    HR   |    FA   |
+----+---------+---------+
|   1|        1|        1|
+----+---------+---------+
|   2|   0.9992|        0|
+----+---------+---------+
END>
Training until now has taken 0 days 0 hours 0 minutes 33 seconds.

===== TRAINING 3-stage =====
<BEGIN
POS count : consumed   2500 : 2504
```
### Remarks on the generated output
1. How is it possible that from a single window of 66x18 pixels, more then 3 stages of 10.000 negatives can be grabbed? Imagining that the second stages only takes negatives that are not classified as negatives by the previous cascade, this can grow easily up to 40.000 - 50.000 samples just for those 3 stages trained.
2. A known problem is that the training hangs once it cannot find new negative samples. But in my opinion in this case it hangs way to late, and should rather drop an error message saying, `oh hello, I cannot find any more negative windows, do provide me more if you want to continue training`

title: Possible bug / precision issue in normalize()
body:
OpenCV Python version: 4.8.1
Operating system: Windows 10
Python version: 3.12.0
Results differ between OpenCV normalization and one made manually with numpy. Difference persists with float64 as well.
Another issue is that the maximum that the function outputs is larger than the beta parameter that is supplied.
Example code to reproduce:
```python
data = np.array([0, 1.4, 20.003, 103.9983, 240.2342, 15.9687195], dtype=np.float32)

norm_opencv = cv2.normalize(data, None, 0, 255, cv2.NORM_MINMAX)
norm_opencv = np.ravel(norm_opencv)
norm_numpy = (data - np.min(data)) / (np.max(data) - np.min(data)) * 255.0

print(norm_opencv)
print(norm_numpy)
print(np.max(np.abs(norm_opencv-norm_numpy)))
```
Output:
```
[  0.          1.4860499  21.232468  110.39047   255.00002    16.950224 ]
[  0.          1.4860498  21.232468  110.390465  255.         16.950224 ]
1.5258789e-05
```

title: Using Charuco Board to get camera intrinsics in opencv-python
body:
Hi, I am having issues finding updated documentation on the process for getting calibration matrix and dist values in opencv using python. I am a bit new to this process as a whole and was hoping for some guidance and help.
Here I have attached the script I have been trying to build, this is what I have come up with so far with opencv 4.10, however there are still some function calls that don't seem to exist in Aruco anymore. Any help would be appreciated or potentially a sample script would be great!
`import cv2
import os
import numpy as np
import logging
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib as mpl
from cv2 import aruco
#set logging level to info or above
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
#set output path
output = r"C:\Users\patrnih1\Documents\Python_Scripts\Intrinsics\output.txt"
# ENTER YOUR REQUIREMENTS HERE:
class ArucoError(Exception):
pass
def create_aruco(columns, rows, square_length, marker_length, dictionary):
if dictionary == 6:
aruco_dict = cv2.aruco.getPredefinedDictionary(cv2.aruco.DICT_6X6_250)
board = cv2.aruco.CharucoBoard((columns, rows), square_length, marker_length, aruco_dict)
elif dictionary == 4:
aruco_dict = cv2.aruco.getPredefinedDictionary(cv2.aruco.DICT_4X4_50)
board = cv2.aruco.CharucoBoard((columns, rows), square_length, marker_length, aruco_dict)
elif dictionary == 5:
aruco_dict = cv2.aruco.getPredefinedDictionary(cv2.aruco.DICT_5X5_250)
board = cv2.aruco.CharucoBoard((columns, rows), square_length, marker_length, aruco_dict)
elif dictionary == 7:
aruco_dict = cv2.aruco.getPredefinedDictionary(cv2.aruco.DICT_7X7_250)
board = cv2.aruco.CharucoBoard((columns, rows), square_length, marker_length, aruco_dict)
else:
raise ArucoError("Invalid dictionary")
pass
imboard = board.generateImage((2000, 200))
cv2.imwrite(r"C:\Users\patrnih1\Documents\Python_Scripts\Intrinsics\charuco.png", imboard)
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(1, 1, 1)
plt.imshow(imboard, cmap=mpl.colormaps['Greys'], interpolation="nearest")
ax.axis("off")
plt.show()
return aruco_dict, board
PATH_TO_YOUR_IMAGES = '/Users/Ed/Downloads/Calibration_Images'
def calibrate_and_save_parameters(ARUCO_DICT, board):
# Define the aruco dictionary and charuco board
SQUARES_VERTICALLY = board.size[0]
SQUARES_HORIZONTALLY = board.size[1]
SQUARE_LENGTH = board.squareLength
MARKER_LENGTH = board.markerLength
dictionary = cv2.aruco.getPredefinedDictionary(ARUCO_DICT)
board = cv2.aruco.CharucoBoard((SQUARES_VERTICALLY, SQUARES_HORIZONTALLY), SQUARE_LENGTH, MARKER_LENGTH, dictionary)
params = cv2.aruco.DetectorParameters()
# Load PNG images from folder
image_files = [os.path.join(PATH_TO_YOUR_IMAGES, f) for f in os.listdir(PATH_TO_YOUR_IMAGES) if f.endswith(".png")]
image_files.sort()  # Ensure files are in order
all_charuco_corners = []
all_charuco_ids = []
for image_file in image_files:
image = cv2.imread(image_file)
image_copy = image.copy()
marker_corners, marker_ids, _ = cv2.aruco.detectMarkers(image, dictionary, parameters=params)
# If at least one marker is detected
if len(marker_ids) > 0:
cv2.aruco.drawDetectedMarkers(image_copy, marker_corners, marker_ids)
charuco_retval, charuco_corners, charuco_ids = cv2.aruco.interpolateCornersCharuco(marker_corners, marker_ids, image, board)
if charuco_retval:
all_charuco_corners.append(charuco_corners)
all_charuco_ids.append(charuco_ids)
# Calibrate camera
retval, camera_matrix, dist_coeffs, rvecs, tvecs = cv2.aruco.calibrateCameraCharuco(all_charuco_corners, all_charuco_ids, board, image.shape[:2], None, None)
# Save calibration data
np.save('camera_matrix.npy', camera_matrix)
np.save('dist_coeffs.npy', dist_coeffs)
fx = camera_matrix[0, 0]
fy = camera_matrix[1, 1]
cx = camera_matrix[0, 2]
cy = camera_matrix[1, 2]
distortion = dist_coeffs.flatten()
with open(output, "w") as f:
f.write("fx: {0}\n".format(fx))
logging.info("fx: {0}".format(fx))
f.write("fy: {0}\n".format(fy))
logging.info("fy: {0}".format(fy))
f.write("cx: {0}\n".format(cx))
logging.info("cx: {0}".format(cx))
f.write("cy: {0}\n".format(cy))
logging.info("cy: {0}".format(cy))
for k in range(len(distortion)):
f.write("k{0}: {1}\n".format(k+1, distortion[k]))
logging.info("k{0}: {1}".format(k+1, distortion[k]))
# Iterate through displaying all the images
for image_file in image_files:
image = cv2.imread(image_file)
undistorted_image = cv2.undistort(image, camera_matrix, dist_coeffs)
cv2.imshow('Undistorted Image', undistorted_image)
cv2.waitKey(0)
cv2.destroyAllWindows()
calibrate_and_save_parameters()`

title: Issues with memory leaks in OpenCV 3.1.0
body:
We are testing our own code with boost tests and we need to check the unit tests for memory leaks. Meanwhile I patched OpenCV 3.1.0 at several positions to remove memory leaks:
1. The instance management in macro CV_SINGLETON_LAZY_INIT_ in precomp.hpp changed from
static TYPE\* volatile instance = NULL;
to
static std::unique_ptr<TYPE> instance;
2. This is a bug for me, complete unexpected behavior, and got a lot of rofl in our software department:
cv::__termination = true
Please remove this flag from the code! Why should one disable memory free's when unloading the library???
3. Now I'm searching for two more memory leaks. One seems to be a pointer (4 byte on 32 bit systems and 8 byte on 64 bit systems) and the other one a 48 byte object.
If somebody knows about the last two objects please answer and spare me a lot of work to find them.
Thank you.

title: cmake, make -j4 and sudo make install are successful but still can't import cv2
body:
So I ran all these commands successfully however still cv2 cannot be imported. Any thought or fix is really appreciated in prior:
```
 ~  c   master ⭑  Face_Recognition  ls                                                                                                  10:03:38 PM
3.1.0.zip            3.1.0.zip.1          opencv-3.1.0         opencv_contrib-3.1.0 papers
-------------------------------------------------------  

In opencv-3.1.0/modules/viz/src/vtk/vtkCocoaInteractorFix.mm
#if VTK_MAJOR_VERSION >= 6 && VTK_MINOR_VERSION >=2

If we use VTK 7.0.0, although it is a newer version, the minor version is less than two, so the vtkCocoaInteractorFix.mm will handle this as older VTK versions and that causes the problem.

So to fix this we just need to add the version number 7 to the line.

#if VTK_MAJOR_VERSION >= 6 && VTK_MINOR_VERSION >=2 || VTK_MAJOR_VERSION >=7
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
cmake -D CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=RELEASE \
      -D CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/usr/local \
      -D BUILD_opencv_java=OFF \
      -D WITH_IPP=OFF -D WITH_1394=OFF \
      -D WITH_FFMPEG=OFF\
      -D BUILD_EXAMPLES=ON \
      -D BUILD_TESTS=ON \
      -D BUILD_PERF_TESTS=OFF \

      -D BUILD_opencv_python3=ON \
```

title: A newSelf-Calibrated Illumination (SCI) learning framework
body:
### Descripe the feature and motivation
We develop a newSelf-Calibrated Illumination (SCI) learning framework for fast, flexible, androbust brightening images in real-world low-light scenarios. To be specific, weestablish a cascaded illumination learning process with weight sharing tohandle this task. Considering the computational burden of the cascaded pattern,we construct the self-calibrated module which realizes the convergence betweenresults of each stage, producing the gains that only use the single basic blockfor inference (yet has not been exploited in previous works), which drasticallydiminishes computation cost. We then define the unsupervised training loss toelevate the model capability that can adapt to general scenes. We think this method can be well introduced into OpenCV because of its lightweight.
The source code is available at https://github.com/vis-opt-group/SCI.

```model = tf.keras.models.Sequential([
  tf.keras.layers.MaxPooling2D(),
  tf.keras.layers.Conv2D(64, 3, padding='same', activation='relu'),
  tf.keras.layers.MaxPooling2D(),
  tf.keras.layers.Flatten(),
  tf.keras.layers.Dense(128, activation='relu'),
  tf.keras.layers.Dense(2)
])

model.compile(optimizer='adam',
              loss=tf.keras.losses.SparseCategoricalCrossentropy(from_logits=True),
              metrics=['accuracy'])

model.fit(
  train_ds,
  validation_data=val_ds,
  epochs=10
)

model.save('./')
model = cv.dnn.readNetFromTensorflow('saved_model.pb')
```
forum.opencv.org, Stack Overflow, etc and have not found any solution

title: fastNlMeansDenoisingMulti does not support 16 bit values
body:
OpenCV version: 4.10.0
Operating System / Platform: All
Compiler & compiler version: All
The documentation for [fastNlMeansDenoisingMulti](https://docs.opencv.org/4.10.0/d1/d79/group__photo__denoise.html#ga723ffde1969430fede9241402e198151) states that it should support 16 bit values.
The [implementation](https://github.com/opencv/opencv/blob/3d91d75f1a48dbba46f8cb7f264485a37e6102a2/modules/photo/src/denoising.cpp#L244) however, enforces 8 bit values even though the underlying code supports 16 bit.
A fix is available in [my branch](https://github.com/opencv/opencv/compare/4.x...dchansen:opencv:nlMeansFix)
1. Run fastNlMeansDenoisingMulti with a 16 bit image.
2. Get error

title: Calibration simulation gives wrong answer
body:
- OpenCV => 3.3.0
- Operating System / Platform => Windows 64 Bit
- Compiler => Visual Studio 2012
Hello,
I'm investigating influence of calibration target on accuracy of calibration. When points are organized in a 3D pattern, calib3d uses DLT method for initial estimation of extrinsics. Without noise (in projection of calibration target features) estimation works fine. With small random Gaussian noise, occasionally the estimate is wrong and result is very different from true values. With increasing noise percentage of "bad" results grows. This never happens when all target points are planar, but then calib3d does not use DLT...
The issue is not repeatable, as it depends on the randomizing seed, but typical ratio of "bad" estimates is:
Noise   Percentage
0.01     0.0
0.03    3.6
0.05    6.3
0.07   10.6
0.1     15.4
Any ideas why DLT may fail?
Yuri

Hi, I have some CNN which outputs bounding boxes for screws. I need to find position and orientation of screw inside every box (or confirm that the box is empty).  My idea was to apply Canny and find the outer contour with proper area and number of corners/compare with some template using cv2.matchShapes().
